title: The No-Nonsense Guide to How Bangladeshi Freelancers Accept International Payments
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: As a Bangladeshi freelancer, you face significant challenges in accepting international payments due to the country's restrictions on PayPal and other conventional payment processors. With a growing demand for digital products, you're forced to find alternative solutions to get paid for your work. This guide will walk you through a straightforward method to accept international payments, using a blockchain-based payment system that circumvents traditional banking limitations.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The geographic exclusion that creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh face from conventional payment platforms is not the result of individual decisions — it is systemic. PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad all operate within the traditional financial system, which means they are subject to the same correspondent banking relationships, compliance requirements, and risk frameworks that make international money movement difficult in certain corridors. Blockchain-based payments operate outside this system entirely. A USDT transaction from a buyer in Germany to a seller in Lagos does not pass through any correspondent bank. It does not require a compliance officer to approve the corridor. It settles peer-to-peer, on-chain, in under 60 seconds.
What You Need Before You Start

To get started, you'll need to sign up for a free account on the NOWPayments platform, which integrates with the Web3 Wallet Connect frontend wallet integration. This will give you access to a wide range of cryptocurrencies, including BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more. Make sure to also set up a payment address and configure your wallet settings accordingly.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

GitHub Pages provides free, reliable hosting for static sites with no server management required. A digital store built on GitHub Pages with the Core Shop Engine has no monthly hosting cost, scales automatically to any traffic level, and can be deployed from anywhere in the world. The combination of free hosting, crypto payments through NOWPayments, and automated delivery through the Core Shop Engine creates a store infrastructure with a total cost structure that is fundamentally different from platform-based alternatives. There are no percentage fees on sales, no monthly subscription costs, and no platform that can decide to delist your products based on content policy changes.
You can now accept international payments and sell digital products without needing a traditional bank account.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live with your digital product store. Use the Admin Dashboard to monitor your transactions, manage orders, and track your sales in real-time. To make your first sale, simply add your products to the store, set the prices, and share the link with your clients. With a secure and reliable payment system in place, you can confidently accept international payments and grow your business.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

As your business grows, you can scale your payment and delivery pipeline by integrating more features and functionalities. Consider upgrading to the Full Stack Bundle, which includes priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide. With this bundle, you'll have access to a dedicated support team and a wide range of resources to help you optimize your payment system and expand your business.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

Yes, by using a blockchain-based payment system, you can accept international payments and sell digital products without needing a traditional bank account. This method eliminates the need for intermediaries and costly fees, making it easier for you to get paid for your work.
